pages.sceneTitle: The Boy at the Clean Table

beats.text: But the boy sitting at the bird-poopless table looked ... normal. He was reading the newspaper and twirling a red pen in his fingers. He had narrow shoulders and a head full of messy-spiky blond hair that reminded me of a crown. He didn't look at me.

speech.speaker: narrator
speech.text: But the boy sitting at the bird-poopless table looked ... normal. He was reading the newspaper and twirling a red pen in his fingers. He had narrow shoulders and a head full of messy-spiky blond hair that reminded me of a crown. He didn't look at me.

beats.staging: 8-second video, 16:9. A quiet school courtyard in a small Southern town. Several outdoor lunch tables are messy with bird droppings, but one table is strangely clean. A shy girl notices it and stops. At the clean table, a slim blond boy reads a folded newspaper and casually twirls a red pen. Camera starts wide, then slowly pushes in from the girl's point of view. Mood: curious, ordinary but slightly uncanny. Audio: distant schoolyard ambience, paper rustle, soft pen tapping.

8-second video, 16:9. The girl steps closer to the clean lunch table, nervous but determined. The boy keeps reading the newspaper, not looking at her, the red pen spinning between his fingers. Over-the-shoulder camera from behind the girl. She whispers, "Pumpernickel?" The pen stops mid-twirl, and the courtyard sound gently fades. Mood: quiet tension, comic awkwardness, first contact.

beats.text: The boy glanced up then. His eyes were the greenest green I'd ever seen, like somebody had taken a neon marker and colored them in just before I walked up to him and called him a Beedle.

speech.speaker: narrator
speech.text: The boy glanced up then. His eyes were the greenest green I'd ever seen, like somebody had taken a neon marker and colored them in just before I walked up to him and called him a Beedle.

beats.staging: 8-second video, 16:9. Close-up sequence. The boy slowly lifts his head from the newspaper and looks directly at the girl. His eyes are the greenest vivid green, like fresh neon ink, but still gentle and human. Abstract glowing handwritten strokes drift around the girl for a moment, suggesting hidden words and magic. Camera slowly pushes into his eyes. Audio: soft shimmer, paper flutter, held breath. Mood: wonder, surprise, the beginning of friendship.
